channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: NAKWON: Last Paradise Hits 24,000 Players in Testing
item.content:encoded:
NAKWON: Last Paradise is an upcoming post-apocalyptic survival game from NEXON that bears stark similarities to the extraction shooters doing so well out there today. It has been in development for years and has just entered a closed playtest, and by all accounts, it’s doing extremely well for itself.

At the time of writing, NAKWON had managed to secure a peak player count of 24,000 in the playtest, and it’s climbing. The streamers and average gamers picking up the playtest have fed back plenty of positivity about the game, which is fast becoming one of the most anticipated zombie survival games to emerge in recent years.

NAKWON: Last Paradise Soars in Testing

NAKWON: Last Paradise is a zombie survival game set in a South Korean apocalypse. The world has ended by way of a cataclysm that turned many of the world’s residents into bloodthirsty creatures, and it’s up to small pockets of survivors to scrape by in settlements for as long as they can.

Players must venture into the no man’s land around their settlements, fight other users, the undead, and deal with the perils of the apocalypse, all while looting, solving quests, and uncovering the secrets of the end of the world.

It’s brutal, open-ended, and built around customization and choice. Players have a ‘base of operations’ that they can upgrade and build out using resources scavenged from the open world, provided they’re talented enough to escape the undead hordes alive.

NEXON has spent years assembling NAKWON: Last Paradise, and it still doesn’t have a release date, but it does have a closed testing window. At the time of publication, a whopping 24,000 people were neck-deep in NAKWON’s universe, and that number was climbing steadily as more users were accepted into the test.

It’s unique enough to carve out a nice corner of the post-apocalyptic space. By day, players explore their safe zone of ‘Yeouido’, visiting stores, interacting with traders, building their home, and upgrading their skills at practice ranges. By night, they’re in the wild and digging through a harrowing wasteland.

NAKWON isn’t a guns-blazing kind of title, though. Traditional firearms are extremely scarce, if not impossible to find, and those that do exist are cobbled together from scrap parts. Instead, the game relies almost entirely on hand-to-hand combat and melee weapons.

Players must equip shovels, bats, planks, knives, and other assorted handheld weapons to send the undead back down to the depths. In this way, the game also enables a stealthy approach, allowing for takedowns, avoiding fights, and lurking in the shadows, trying not to be seen while carrying a hefty backpack full of loot.

NAKWON: Last Paradise is coming exclusively to PC via Steam, and while it doesn’t have a release date, it would be surprising if it didn’t land this year.

Are you in the NAKWON test? Let us know if you’re enjoying it on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Starfield’s future will be uncovered next week

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Starfield’s Future Will Be Unveiled Next Week by Bethesda
item.content:encoded:
Everyone who loves Starfield is eager to know what’s coming next for the game. Recently, Bethesda Game Studios once again invited select creators (but of course) to exclusive previews for Starfield’s impending ‘overhaul’, giving them a sneak peek at what’s in store for the space-faring RPG epic.

Todd Howard has tempered expectations by saying it’s not Starfield 2.0, but it’s more likely fresh information about Terran Armada, the game’s painfully late second expansion. It’s also going to be more on the massive update that’s supposed to make the game even better for those who already love it.

But If You Didn’t Like It, Tough.

Todd Howard said in a recent interview that, if you didn’t gel with Starfield, the incoming supersized update won’t change that. It’s not about making you fall in love with the game if you didn’t enjoy it in the first place. Instead, it’ll take those who adored the game the first time around and make them even more enthralled by it.

That’s not a lot to go on, but there we go.

Last year, Bethesda Game Studios teased Terran Armada, which everyone believes is the name for the game’s second expansion, which is massively overdue. Bethesda has been awfully short with fans regarding the second expansion, which should have been fully unveiled months ago.

Before launch, we were promised one major expansion a year. The game’s first expansion, Shattered Space, was known about long before the game hit the market in 2023, but until now, all we have regarding the game’s second expansion is a potential name. We’re left to wonder what Terran Armada is, but it’s all based on conjecture.

Now, in a new post on social media, the Bethesda Game Studios team let slip that something is being revealed next week for Starfield. When responding to an article talking about Todd Howard being a ‘visionary’, the team wrote

We ran this by Todd and he said his only visionary power is seeing running lanes in EA College Football 26. He appreciates all the passionate feedback on Starfield and we’ll have more to share next week.

Starfield is preparing to land on PlayStation 5 consoles next month, broadening the game’s horizons immeasurably and opening it up to an entirely new audience. However, some feel that the global expansion is too little, too late, when Bethesda Game Studios has allowed the game to wither under the weight of almost no tangible or meaningful updates over the years.

This could be a turning point for Starfield, though. If the PlayStation 5 launch hits hard, the next expansion is unveiled, and it looks superb, and there’s enough meat on whatever update is coming to entice players back into the fold, it could lead to a resurgence.

That’s sorely needed for Starfield.

Are you looking forward to whatever is coming next for Starfield? Let us know what you’re thinking on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Cyberpunk 2077 is getting no more DLCs

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#1007)—Answers for Saturday, March 14
item.content:encoded:
It’s the weekend! That means it’s time to relax, indulge, and perhaps solve an NYT Connections puzzle or two. If you’re pushing up the numbers on that streak, don’t let today’s challenge weigh you down. Below, I’ve got the NYT Connections hints and answers you need to get today started the right way.

Read on to get what you need to know about today’s NYT Connections puzzle.

How to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (March 14)

Here are the words from today’s NYT Connections puzzle:

  • NIGHTMARE
  • GADGET
  • BINARY
  • HAZE
  • HOOTENANNY
  • LICHEN
  • MORSE
  • DUOLINGO
  • SPELL
  • CLOUSEAU
  • MOSCOW
  • TRANCE
  • DIOXIDE
  • JAVERY
  • DREAM
  • TWILIGHT

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – States you might find yourself in when hypnotized
  • Green – Random words that start with a prefix you’d associate with ‘two’
  • Blue – Traditional inspectors and detectives
  • Purple – Longer words that end with the name of a female animal

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, March 14:

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, March 14 (#1007)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – DREAM, HAZE, SPELL, TRANCE
  • Green – BINARY, DIOXIDE, DUOLINGO, TWILIGHT
  • Blue – CLOUSEAU, GADGET, JAVERT, MORSE
  • Purple – HOOTENANNY, LICHEN, MOSCOW, NIGHTMARE

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Discord server

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/nyt-connections-hints-solutions-march-14-1007/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Call of Duty: Warzone Patch Fixes Several Bugs
item.content:encoded:
Raven Software has revealed small patch notes for the latest Call of Duty: Warzone Season 2 update. The small patch released on Friday, March 13, added a Win Streak progress tracker to Black Ops Royale and fixed several bugs.

The latest update fixed several bugs, including an issue where spectating a teammate who used a Trauma Kit caused their health indicator to blink red. It also fixed a problem where players in the downed state while wingsuiting lost HUD elements after being revived.

Additionally, the update corrects an issue that ejected players seated in the rear when using the Climb to Roof feature on the Polaris. Lastly, it fixed an issue where the Reactive Armor Perk did not regenerate armor after a player was revived from being downed.

More on Call of Duty: Warzone’s Black Ops Royale

Black Ops Royale launched on March 13. The new Battle Royale mode received mixed reactions from players on its first day. However, Raven Software is already addressing player concerns. They have announced that they are working on more ways to play in the Battle Royale mode, and for starters, Solos will be coming to private games very soon.

Those early games will be used to collect data for an eventual Solos launch in Ranked Play. Learn more about their response to players at this link.

How are you enjoying Call of Duty: Warzone? Have you tried Black Ops Royale yet? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

item.title: NYT Strands #740 Hints and Answers for Today, March 13, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #740 for Friday, March 13, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters and under do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints For Today, March 13, 2026

The Strands’ answers to Game #740 all focus on instruments used in Blue Grass by “Mountain Bands.”

General Strands Hints For March 13

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: Knights wear this shiny protective gear.
  • Hint 2: Military servicemembers wear this
  • Hint 3: This is a strong upside to those who are quick.

Use these clues to narrow down your guesses and increase your chances of solving Strands #740 for March 13, 2026.

The Strands’ answers to Game #738 all focus on different defense mechanisms. Not all relate to physical battles, however.

General Strands Hints For March 13

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: Johnny Cash, Elvis Presley, Carlos Santana.
  • Hint 2: Instrument used when portraying a prototypical Hillbilly character.
  • Hint 3: This instrument is featured heavily in “Run Around” by Blues Traveler.

Use these clues to narrow down your guesses and increase your chances of solving Strands #740 for March 13, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for March 13, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • FILE
  • HARM
  • LION
  • BAND
  • BLUE

Today’s Strands Answer For Game #740 On March 13, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • HARMONICA
  • MANDOLIN
  • BANJO
  • FIDDLE
  • GUITAR
  • SPANAGRAM: BLUEGRASSMUSIC

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #740?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on March 14 will be Strands Game #741.

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett serves as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since its launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/nyt-strands-740-hints-and-answers-for-today-march-13-2026/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Cody Rhodes and Roman Reigns Were Kept Apart on ‘Street Fighter’ Set
item.content:encoded:
Cody Rhodes and Roman Reigns are two of the top names in WWE currently. They headlined two WrestleManias in a row, facing off for the WWE Championship. The WWE Superstars are also two cast members in the upcoming Street Fighter film. Rhodes is playing Guile while Reigns is playing Akuma.

Given their history, some fans wondered what it would look like when they crossed paths on the big screen in the upcoming film based on the Capcom franchise. However, Cody Rhodes is now revealing that he and Roman were intentionally kept apart on set.

Cody Rhodes Thinks Film Crew Thought He and Roman Reigns Would Fight On Set

In a new interview with Chris Van Vliet, Cody Rhodes says he and Roman Reigns were only on set together one day, and they had a very mundane interaction.

Cody also claims that he believes Roman would corroborate his belief that those on-set thought they might actually fight for real.

“We had one day on set, and the way the set was, and he could probably reiterate this — I think they thought we were going to fight or have issues,” he said. “They were very cognizant on the radios of [saying], ‘Stepping out with Guile.’ [and] ‘Akuma will be coming on.’ They were very cognizant that we would not cross paths. We did, because I had to tell him the direction of one of the trailers. That was our only moment, and it was a very awkward, like the weirdest moment.”

Roman Reigns and Cody Rhodes are not the only cast members who are World Champion professional wrestlers. Hirooki Goto, a New Japan Pro-Wrestling star, will be playing E. Honda. The movie will be coming out this October. Insider Gaming will continue to provide the latest updates on the film.

Do you think Roman Reigns and Cody Rhodes should have interacted on-screen? Are you excited for the upcoming Street Fighter movie? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Embark CEO Discusses AI Usage in ARC Raiders
item.content:encoded:
Embark Studios CEO Patrick Söderlund discusses how AI was used in the creation of ARC Raiders.

ARC Raiders continues to take the World by storm and is a rare modern example of a live-service game that has found an audience and maintained a consistent player count. AI as a technology is also trying to find its place in the gaming industry.

In general, players are wary of AI, but some studios feel it can be a useful tool early in game development.

In an interview with GamesIndustry.Biz, Söderlund explained that Embark sees technology as a tool. Specifically, AI was used for some of the voice lines in ARC Raiders. Embark says it always works with real actors, but in select cases, it pays them to license their voices for text-to-speech for certain lines. However, certain lines have been re-recorded, and there are currently fewer AI-generated lines in the game than there were at its initial release.

“There is a quality difference. A real professional actor is better than AI; that’s just how it is,” he said. “We look at [AI] first and foremost as a production tool. We can test things internally. We can test 15 different lines without recording them, and then we know what to record. It’s also a way for us to work, not replace actors. We don’t necessarily believe in replacing humans with AI all the time.”

In other AI-related gaming news, Xbox’s new CEO, Asha Sharma, has given her stance on “bad AI.” She previously worked in Microsoft’s AI department. Read her comments here.

How do you feel about Patrick Söderlund’s comments regarding AI? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Raven Software Confirms Solos Coming to Call of Duty: Black Ops Royale
item.content:encoded:
Raven Software has responded to community feedback and confirmed that Solos will be added to Call of Duty: Warzone’s Black Ops Royale as a Private Match option sometime next week.

Call of Duty: Black Ops Royale is now available to play. Now, Raven Software is responding to players who want more ways to play the mode, announcing that Solos are coming soon.

Solos Comes to Call of Duty: Black Ops Royale Next Week

In a tweet, Raven Software explained that Solos will be added as a Private Match option within the next week to help the overall balance and gather early data. Some of the comments were receptive to this news. Other comments, however, argued that these modes should have been considered before release.

“While this will require some backend work and a few changes that need to happen before it goes live, we’re excited to get Solos into the game, and we’ll share more details on timing as we get closer to deploying. We plan to add Solos as a Private Match option next week to help gather early data on match pacing and overall balance,” Raven Software said.

Players can learn more about the new game mode here. For more Call of Duty on Insider Gaming, check out the season 2 rode map for Call of Duty Black Ops 7 at this link.

Have you played this game mode yet? What do you think of Black Ops Royale? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

item.title: League of Legends Voice Chat Confirmed But Not Any Time Soon
item.content:encoded:
Riot Games has confirmed that voice chat is coming to League of Legends, but it may not be for quite some time.

Dataminers discovered files for LoL voice chat on the PBE earlier this year, yet Riot had not officially commented until now.

The publisher has released a dev blog revealing that team-based voice comms will indeed be added to the game, but not until developers believe the feature is in a good place with a lower risk of toxicity.

Riot claims: “We think when done well, team voice chat opens up the doors for enhanced teamwork and coordination, and has the potential to improve the overall gameplay experience for players that would like to use it.”

The blog adds: “We’ll do this by working with you all to find the best version of team voice chat for the League community.”

Riot Addresses LoL Voice Chat Concerns

Riot Games has been working hard to combat the toxic reputation of the game and has been cracking down on bad behaviour for a while now.

Despite this, the publisher seemingly doesn’t think that LoL is ready for voice chat yet as the dev blog states: “We want to get this right, so it isn’t going to happen fast.”

Riot claims it hadn’t added voice chat sooner because it didn’t meet the publisher’s standards for safety and reliability, while now technology has advanced enough to “identify bad actors.”

Riot acknowledged concerns over voice chat toxicity, stating:

“We hear that you are worried about a world where some people use Team Voice to be jerks, but we want to reassure you: we’re listening to your concerns and we are committed to making sure that that world does not come to pass.”

The dev blog claims that developers will meet with players around the world to test the new feature and suggests it may roll out one language or region at a time.

Currently, Riot states there is no timeline as to when voice chat will be added, but says more details will be shared in another dev blog later this year.

Do you think voice chat will be good for LoL or make it even more toxic? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

The publishers are being kept busy as LoL, TFT and other Riot titles have been banned for under-18s in Brazil.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.
